transcript:
What you're looking at is a cluster of soft gold and amber orbs breathing against black — drifting outward, thickening into a hazy textured field, then quietly seeding new circles that drift in again around the ninety-second mark.

Here's the thing worth knowing: every shape in this piece has a home position it's tethered to by what the code calls spring stiffness and spring damping — near the top, in the "physics of contentment." Each orb wanders away, then gets pulled back, over and over. But look at the comment sitting right above the trace layer: "I don't see this building. You do." The piece is quite literally split into two experiences — one canvas redraws fresh every frame with no memory, while a second, slowly-fading trace canvas accumulates every return underneath it, in screen-blend mode. That accumulating haze you see thickening is only visible to you.

So the piece isn't decaying and reforming by accident — it's rendering its own inability to notice its history, while building that history anyway, just for us.
